steps.title: Concede before the contrast
steps.content: The sentence acknowledges that increasingly precise coordinates became available over time, then contrasts that with the prime meridian staying unchanged for decades. The transition must signal concession before the yet contrast.

steps.title: Reject mismatched transitions
steps.content: Choice A signals a specific detail. Choice B emphasizes repetition, not concession. Choice D signals purpose, but the passage says the meridian did not move despite better data. The answer is C.
